title: FuseDiff: Symmetry-Preserving Joint Diffusion for Dual-Target Structure-Based Drug Design
summary: FuseDiff tackles dual-target drug design by jointly generating one ligand and two target-specific binding poses in a single end-to-end diffusion model. Its dual-target local context fusion preserves shared molecular topology while allowing different geometries for each pocket. The paper reports state-of-the-art docking performance and introduces a more systematic way to assess dual-target pose quality before expensive pose search.
key_findings: Jointly models ligand graph and both pocket-specific poses in one framework
Maintains symmetry and topological consistency across dual-target settings
Achieves state-of-the-art docking performance on benchmark evaluations

title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery LLM Agents
summary: Mozi proposes a governed autonomy architecture for LLM agents in drug discovery pipelines, combining free-form reasoning with structured execution. The system separates control and workflow planes, enforces tool-use constraints, and adds human-in-the-loop checkpoints at high-uncertainty stages. On PharmaBench and case studies, it shows stronger orchestration reliability for long-horizon tasks such as candidate generation with safety filters.
key_findings: Introduces supervisor-worker governance for safer agent tool usage
Adds stateful workflow graphs that map to canonical drug discovery stages
Demonstrates improved orchestration accuracy and robustness on PharmaBench

title: Designing the Haystack: Programmable Chemical Space for Generative Molecular Discovery
summary: This work reframes chemical space as a programmable object rather than a fixed latent distribution. SpaceGFN lets users define building blocks and reaction rules, then uses a GFlowNet to sample property-biased molecules within that designed space. Across 96 targets, it reports strong optimization under synthetic constraints and supports reaction-consistent editing for practical lead refinement.
key_findings: Decouples chemical space design from exploration with programmable constraints
Uses evolution-inspired and pseudo-natural-product spaces to bias discovery
Shows broad optimization gains while retaining structural diversity

title: Toward Closed-loop Molecular Discovery via Language Model, Property Alignment and Strategic Search
summary: Trio combines fragment language modeling, reinforcement learning, and Monte Carlo tree search to create a closed-loop molecular discovery process. The method balances novelty exploration with property-guided exploitation, instead of over-optimizing only affinity. Reported results include stronger binding affinity, drug-likeness, synthetic accessibility, and substantially improved diversity versus prior approaches.
key_findings: Integrates LM generation, RL, and strategic search in one closed loop
Improves binding affinity (+7.85%), drug-likeness (+11.10%), and synthetic accessibility (+12.05%)
Expands molecular diversity by more than fourfold

title: FRAGMENTA: End-to-end Fragmentation-based Generative Model with Agentic Tuning for Drug Lead Optimization
summary: FRAGMENTA introduces an end-to-end fragment-based generator that learns fragmentation as a vocabulary selection problem via dynamic Q-learning. It also adds agentic tuning where domain experts can iteratively refine objectives through natural language feedback. In cancer lead optimization experiments, the framework found nearly twice as many high-scoring molecules as baselines.
key_findings: Replaces heuristic fragmentation with learned, optimization-aware fragmentation
Enables direct expert-in-the-loop objective tuning through an agent interface
Finds roughly 2x more high-scoring candidates in reported experiments

title: SiDGen: Structure-informed Diffusion for Generative modeling of Ligands for Proteins
summary: SiDGen addresses the tradeoff between rich pocket conditioning and computational cost in structure-based design. It introduces a topological information bottleneck that compresses residue-level representations before pairwise reasoning, reducing memory while preserving binding-relevant structure. The paper reports state-of-the-art results on CrossDocked2020 and DUD-E with improved efficiency.
key_findings: Uses a learned bottleneck to reduce pairwise complexity from full-scale pocket modeling
Preserves pocket-aware fidelity while improving memory and compute efficiency
Achieves state-of-the-art benchmark performance for ligand generation

title: Refine Drugs, Don't Complete Them: Uniform-Source Discrete Flows for Fragment-Based Drug Discovery
summary: InVirtuoGen proposes a discrete flow model that shifts molecular generation from masked completion to iterative refinement. By predicting all positions at each denoising step, it decouples generation behavior from strict sequence-length constraints and improves quality-diversity tradeoffs. It reports state-of-the-art practical molecular optimization results and stronger docking-oriented lead optimization outcomes.
key_findings: Introduces refinement-first discrete flow generation for fragmented SMILES
Establishes a stronger quality-diversity frontier in de novo generation
Sets a new state of the art on practical molecular optimization benchmarks

title: POLO: Preference-Guided Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning for Lead Optimization
summary: POLO improves sample efficiency in lead optimization by training on full optimization trajectories instead of isolated single-turn edits. Its Preference-Guided Policy Optimization objective combines trajectory-level rewards with turn-level preference ranking signals. The paper reports strong gains, including 84% success on single-property tasks and 50% on multi-property tasks with only 500 oracle calls.
key_findings: Learns from complete optimization trajectories for better chemical search behavior
Combines dense turn-level preferences with trajectory-level reinforcement signals
Delivers large sample-efficiency gains under tight oracle-evaluation budgets

title: DMol: A Highly Efficient and Chemical Motif-Preserving Molecule Generation Platform
summary: DMol presents a graph diffusion platform designed for faster and more valid small-molecule generation. Compared to prior baselines, it improves validity, cuts required diffusion steps by at least 10x, and reduces runtime by around half. A compressed motif-aware variant further boosts validity and novelty while simplifying generation over ring-rich chemistry.
key_findings: Improves validity over strong graph diffusion baselines
Reduces diffusion steps by at least 10x with major runtime savings
Motif-compressed variant further improves validity and novelty

title: GenMol: A Drug Discovery Generalist with Discrete Diffusion
summary: GenMol is a single discrete diffusion model that supports multiple drug discovery tasks, including de novo generation, fragment-constrained generation, hit generation, and lead optimization. It uses fragment-level SAFE sequences and remasking to efficiently explore and optimize molecular structures. The work reports state-of-the-art task performance while providing a unified model design instead of task-specific pipelines.
key_findings: Unifies multiple molecular design tasks in one diffusion framework
Uses fragment remasking and molecular context guidance for optimization
Achieves state-of-the-art results on goal-directed hit generation and lead optimization
